name: Clear Lake
description:
Clear Lake is the largest natural freshwater lake entirely within California and one of the oldest lakes in North America. Known as the Bass Capital of the West, it consistently produces trophy largemouth bass and hosts numerous professional bass tournaments.

The lake's shallow, warm waters and abundant tule habitat create ideal conditions for largemouth bass exceeding 10 pounds. Spring fishing is legendary, with pre-spawn bass staging along the many points and flats. Multiple public launches around the lake provide access to different basins and fishing zones.

name: Lake Shasta
description:
Lake Shasta is California's largest reservoir, formed by Shasta Dam at the confluence of the Sacramento, Pit, and McCloud rivers in northern California. The lake's 370 miles of shoreline create an enormous fishing playground with diverse habitats.

Trout fishing is outstanding year-round, with rainbow and brown trout growing to impressive sizes in the cold, deep water. Spotted bass have emerged as a premier warm-water target, with the lake producing state-record specimens. Multiple marinas and public ramps provide access throughout the lake's many arms.

name: Lake Oroville
description:
Lake Oroville is one of the deepest reservoirs in California, created by the tallest dam in the United States. The lake occupies dramatic canyon terrain in the Sierra Nevada foothills and serves as a key component of the State Water Project.

Spotted bass are the star attraction, with Oroville consistently producing tournament-winning catches. The lake also supports excellent king salmon fishing in the open water and quality trout in the deeper sections. Water levels can fluctuate dramatically, but the fishing remains productive across conditions.

name: Sacramento River
description:
The Sacramento River is California's longest river and the backbone of the Central Valley's fishery, flowing 400 miles from Mount Shasta to the Sacramento-San Joaquin Delta. The upper river below Keswick Dam is a blue-ribbon trout stream.

Fall-run Chinook salmon attract thousands of anglers from September through November, with fish averaging 15-25 pounds. Winter steelhead provide cold-weather action. The lower river and Delta support striped bass, sturgeon, and shad runs. Numerous public ramps along the river's length provide boat access.

name: San Francisco Bay
description:
San Francisco Bay is one of the world's great natural harbors and one of California's most productive fishing estuaries. The bay's mix of salt and fresh water creates ideal habitat for a remarkable diversity of fish species.

Striped bass and halibut are the primary targets, with the summer halibut bite drawing large crowds. Sturgeon fishing during winter months can produce fish exceeding 200 pounds. Public launch ramps throughout the bay provide access from multiple directions, and the bay's bridge structures create fish-holding habitat.

name: San Diego Bay
description:
San Diego Bay is Southern California's finest natural harbor and a year-round fishing destination with warm-water species rarely found elsewhere on the coast. The bay provides both sheltered fishing and access to the open Pacific.

Spotted bay bass are the bay's signature species, with catch-and-release anglers targeting these aggressive fish around docks, eelgrass beds, and rocky structure. The bay also serves as the launch point for offshore trips targeting yellowtail, tuna, and dorado in the nearby waters of the Pacific.

name: Channel Islands
description:
The Channel Islands off the Southern California coast offer some of the best saltwater fishing on the Pacific Coast. The islands create nutrient-rich upwellings that attract a remarkable variety of game fish from kelp-dwelling calico bass to pelagic yellowtail.

Sportfishing boats depart from Ventura, Oxnard, and Santa Barbara harbors for trips ranging from half-day rockfish excursions to multi-day island camping and fishing adventures. White seabass and yellowtail runs from spring through fall draw dedicated anglers, while bottom fishing for rockfish and lingcod is productive year-round.

name: Lake Tahoe (CA Side)
description:
Lake Tahoe is one of the deepest and clearest lakes in the world, straddling the California-Nevada border at 6,225 feet elevation. The California side offers stunning mountain scenery and excellent cold-water fishing.

Lake trout (mackinaw) up to 30 pounds patrol the deep water and are targeted by trolling and jigging. Rainbow and brown trout provide action closer to shore, especially during spring and fall. Public launches at South Lake Tahoe, Meeks Bay, and Tahoe City provide California-side access.

name: Castaic Lake
description:
Castaic Lake in the hills north of Los Angeles is a legendary trophy bass fishery that once held the world record for largemouth bass. The lake's cold, clear water from the State Water Project and deep canyon structure create ideal conditions for growing giant bass.

The lake record largemouth exceeds 21 pounds, and double-digit bass are caught every season. Trout stocking provides excellent winter fishing and serves as forage for the big bass. Despite its proximity to Los Angeles, the lake offers a wilderness feel in the steep canyon terrain.

name: Don Pedro Reservoir
description:
Don Pedro Reservoir is a large foothill reservoir on the Tuolumne River in the Sierra Nevada foothills east of Modesto. The lake's diverse habitat includes rocky points, submerged timber, and deep creek channels that support an outstanding multi-species fishery.

All three bass species thrive here, with spotted bass being the most abundant. The lake also supports excellent kokanee and king salmon fishing in open water. Spring and fall are prime fishing seasons, though the lower elevation location keeps the lake productive year-round.
